content: When adding per-case recovery to a pipeline (catch -> record a failure result instead of aborting the run), the catch must cover only transient/runtime errors (API 429/5xx, malformed model output, hung subprocess). Deterministic, pre-flight errors — missing/wrong-typed dataset fields, prompt/template-contract mismatches, bad config — must be validated up front or re-raised so they fail the whole run loudly. Absorbing them produces a normal-looking low-scoring report that masks a configuration bug. Pattern: validate the schema before the worker pool (presence AND type), keep a small _CONFIG_ERRORS tuple the guard re-raises, and only then catch Exception for the genuinely flaky steps.
context: je-dev-skills PR #7 (live_run per-case resilience) took 3 Codex P2 rounds, all the same root issue: the broad except that isolated transient executor/judge failures was also swallowing dataset-schema errors (missing prompt_inputs, prompt_inputs: null) and prompt/cases contract errors (MissingPlaceholderError). Fix: up-front _validate_cases (presence+type) + re-raise _CONFIG_ERRORS, catch Exception only around execute()/grade().
title: Isolate only transient errors; let deterministic config/schema/contract errors fail loudly
